title: Nexus sub-agent return envelope
description: Typed, versioned structure for a dispatched persona's return (docs/agents/CONTRACT.md 'Required Output'). F1-06 (nexus-foundation/plans/wave-1.md track c): this schema is the FUTURE load-bearing routing signal that supersedes by-eye parsing of the '## NEXUS:*' marker string. ADDITIVE ONLY in wave-1 — no live gate reads it yet (that is F1-07's dual-parse shadow); it exists so F1-07 hooks and the broker can both validate against one artifact. TWO TIERS (CONTRACT.md line 64-66, DEC-039): the LEAN tier (T0/T1) returns ONLY completion_marker + files_changed + verification_result and carries NO `status` field; the FULL tier (T2 / error-fix / risky) carries `status` plus the marker-class-specific fields. Presence of `status` is therefore the tier discriminator — a status-less envelope is validated as LEAN (verification_result required), a status-bearing envelope as FULL (marker-consistency + per-class requirements enforced via allOf). Only `completion_marker` and `files_changed` are universally required — the two fields CONTRACT.md's LEAN and FULL Required Output both mandate; `completion_marker` is the routing signal in BOTH tiers (CONTRACT.md line 66). Every OTHER field is grounded in either CONTRACT.md 'Required Output' or a key that .claude/hooks/return-validator.py / gate_runner.py actually extract; a field with no such grounding (e.g. a free 'summary') is NEVER required here.

schema_version.description: OPTIONAL envelope schema version. When present it MUST be const 1 for this shape (an unrecognized version — e.g. 2 — is REJECTED, never best-effort parsed); a future field-shape change bumps this and ships a sibling vN schema file, mirroring the node-contract schema_version convention (CONTRACT.md Node-Contract Schema v2). ABSENCE is legal and additive: a pre-versioning envelope (every current CONTRACT.md Required Output example omits this field) and every LEAN-tier return validate without it — requiring it would reject today's conformant envelopes, violating F1-06's ADDITIVE-ONLY mandate. F1-08 may promote it to required at cutover.

status.description: The typed routing signal. Enum covers the FULL current completion-marker vocabulary (CONTRACT.md 'Completion Markers'). This is the field F1-08 makes authoritative in place of regex-matching completion_marker.

completion_marker.description: The literal H2 marker string CONTRACT.md mandates ('## NEXUS:DONE' etc.), kept during the shadow/cutover transition so the typed status can be cross-checked against the human-readable marker. Consistency with `status` is enforced by the allOf branches below (a mismatch is a CONTRACT VIOLATION per CONTRACT.md).

files_changed.description: Paths the agent wrote this dispatch (CONTRACT.md Required Output). Array of non-empty path strings; empty array is legal for a read-only/no-write return (e.g. NEEDS-DECISION).

summary.description: OPTIONAL one-paragraph human-readable outcome summary. NOT a CONTRACT.md Required Output field and NOT extracted by return-validator.py / gate_runner.py (gate_runner's internal 'summary' variables are unrelated review-adapter locals) — it is permitted because some harness StructuredOutput returns include it, but it is NEVER required: a return following CONTRACT.md's canonical Required Output template (which has no 'summary') MUST validate. Grounding an earlier required-summary claim in CONTRACT.md was incorrect (F1-06 REVISE finding #1).

skills_loaded.description: Skills actually invoked this dispatch (CONTRACT.md field notes; ADVISORY/shadow cross-check by lens-fast against skill_load_events).

verification_result.description: The load-bearing completion evidence (CONTRACT.md Rule 3). Normally the VERBATIM terminal output of every verification_required command (string). May instead be an object for the visual-evidence case (screenshot_before / screenshot_after / visual_skip_reason, CONTRACT.md Rule 13). return-validator.py treats presence of this key as proof the agent attempted verification; the schema is stricter — an EMPTY string is not evidence. `minLength` constrains ONLY the string branch of the union (JSON Schema minLength ignores non-strings), so the object/visual-evidence case is unaffected; a substance check on the object branch is deferred to F1-07's dual-parse (F1-06 REVISE finding: the DONE evidence-gate must reject empty-but-present, not just absent).

acceptance_met.description: Per-criterion pass/fail with evidence (CONTRACT.md Required Output; return-validator.py ACCEPTANCE_KEY). Each item pairs the criterion text with a boolean and the line-numbers/output that prove it.

blockers.description: Blocker descriptions (CONTRACT.md; required for status BLOCKED). Each a non-empty description string.

decisions_needed.description: Design choices surfaced mid-task (CONTRACT.md; required for status NEEDS-DECISION). Each carries the question, the options, and the agent's recommendation.

revise_issues.description: The actionable-issue list a '## NEXUS:REVISE' MUST carry (CONTRACT.md 'REVISE issue schema'). Each issue names WHERE (file:line), WHAT (verbatim error), and FIX (concrete change). `line` accepts an integer or a range string ('38-45') or a gate-name string.

defer_request.description: The DEFER-REQUEST body (CONTRACT.md 'NEXUS:DEFER-REQUEST'): what out-of-scope error was found, why it is out of scope, and the inline-vs-defer effort estimate. Required for status DEFER-REQUEST.

db_log_cmds.description: Commands the orchestrator (not the agent) runs to update the memory DB on completion (CONTRACT.md Rule 6).

notepad_written.description: The agent-notepad insight written as the LAST action, OR an explicit skip (CONTRACT.md Rule 17). Omitting this field entirely is a CONTRACT VIOLATION.

notes.description: Anything the orchestrator must know; for status CHECKPOINT this carries the remaining work at the safe resume point.

root_cause_analysis.description: REQUIRED for any error-fix / bug-investigation task (CONTRACT.md Rule 11). The why_chain traces symptom -> genuine root; as many steps as the cause needs, no minimum, no padding.

deploy_step.description: REQUIRED for any delivery touching app/ ingestion/ design/ or docker-compose*.yml (CONTRACT.md Rule 14). Documents the restart action and the command that confirms the new code is running.

evidence.description: StructuredOutput-schema equivalent evidence key that return-validator.py accepts as substantive completion proof (_EXTRA_EVIDENCE_KEYS).

deterministic_evidence.description: StructuredOutput-schema equivalent evidence key that return-validator.py accepts as substantive completion proof (_EXTRA_EVIDENCE_KEYS).

checks.description: StructuredOutput deterministic-verification array; return-validator.py counts a non-empty checks[] whose items carry evidence/notes/result/status as structured proof (_CHECKS_KEY).

allOf.$comment: LEAN tier (CONTRACT.md line 64, DEC-039): a status-less envelope is a LEAN (T0/T1) return and MUST still carry verification_result — completion_marker + files_changed are already universally required, so this branch completes LEAN's three-field contract. `if: not required status` is the tier discriminator: it fires ONLY when `status` is absent, so it never constrains a FULL-tier (status-bearing) return.

status <-> completion_marker consistency (CONTRACT.md 'status <-> completion_marker mapping'; the marker is the human-readable twin of the typed status during transition). Each branch carries `required: [status]` so it fires ONLY for a FULL-tier (status-bearing) return; without that guard a status-less LEAN envelope would trivially match `properties.status` in EVERY branch (properties constrains only present keys) and be forced to satisfy six mutually exclusive completion_marker consts at once — an unsatisfiable contradiction that would wrongly reject every LEAN return (F1-06 REVISE finding #2).

DONE requires the load-bearing verification evidence + per-criterion acceptance (CONTRACT.md Rule 3 + Required Output). Presence alone is insufficient: acceptance_met MUST carry >=1 criterion (mirroring BLOCKED's blockers minItems), and verification_result's own top-level minLength:1 rejects an empty-string green — together they close the empty-but-present evidence bypass (F1-06 REVISE finding).
BLOCKED requires a non-empty blockers list (CONTRACT.md).
REVISE requires the actionable issue list (CONTRACT.md anti-stall mandate).
CHECKPOINT requires remaining-work notes at the safe resume point (CONTRACT.md).
